MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VIVIAN LERCHE, TEL. NO. 1-415-680-5731 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Aaron Chandler's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue precluding summary judgment and summary adjudication whether Mr. Chandler was involved in the automobile accident. (Chandler Dec. pars. 4 and 7; Delapp Dec. pars. 2-3.) Mr. Chandler fails to show that the statute of limitations bars this action. He cites CCP 583.210 and 583.250, but those statutes govern the time limitations in which to serve a defendant. The cited statutes are inapplicable to this action. The court's register of actions (10/4/12 entry) indicates that Mr. Chandler was timely served. The fact that he subsequently prevailed on his motion to vacate default and default judgment does not render service untimely. Even if the service were improper, the timing provisions of CCP 583.210 and 583.250 were tolled during the pendency of the default per CCP 583.240(d). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT AARON CHANDLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant Aaron Chandler's motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied without prejudice to being refiled with proper notice. The motion was filed with insufficient notice. (CCP 437c(a)). Even if the motion were characterized as a motion for judgment on the pleading, it still was filed with insufficient notice. (CCP 1005). The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Mpa; Declaration
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aaron Chandler (510-282-8526) appears as defendant/moving party, In Propria Persona

No other appearance is made in this matter.

DEFENDANT AARON CHANDLER'S Motion To Set Aside Default entered November 26, 2012 And Default Judgment entered August 21, 2013 is GRANTED. The applications for relief under CCP 473 and 473.5 are untimely. However, defendant's papers establish good cause for equitable relief: (1) his declaration states a meritorious defense, (2) defendant's unawareness of the lawsuit is a satisfactory excuse for not defending himself and (3) defendant diligently filed this motion within weeks of learning of the action. (See Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 982.) The proposed answer is to be filed immediately. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
